widgets_values:
VLM SCALE CUES -- automatic reference-object detection via a local vision LLM

Requires a running local vision-capable model. This file is preset for
LM Studio serving google/gemma-4-12b-qat at the default port (1234) --
change provider/model/base_url widgets on AtlasVLMScaleCues for ollama
(default model gemma3:4b, port 11434) or llamacpp (port 8080) instead.

NOTE on the LM Studio path (fixed 2026-07-04): earlier this always failed
silently with 'model not available' because LM Studio's own /api/v1/models
endpoint uses "key" as the model identifier field, not "id"/"model"/"name"
-- atlas_camera/inference/multimodal_helper.py now checks "key" first.
If you're on an older checkout, update atlas_camera first.

KNOWN LIMITATION (verified via direct testing, not a code bug): quantized
local vision models -- confirmed with Gemma 4 12B QAT (Q4) -- can be
unreliable at structured JSON+bbox extraction: it may hallucinate field
names outside the requested schema, or degenerate into a repeated-key
decoding loop mid-response (the code detects and truncates repetition
loops, but the salvaged partial JSON still may not parse). If
scale_references comes back empty ([]) despite an obvious reference object
in frame, that's this limitation, not a broken pipeline -- fall back to
AtlasReferenceScaleSolve (see atlas_camera_reference_scale_workflow.json)
with a manually-drawn bbox, or try a larger/less-quantized vision model.

AtlasApplyScaleReferences' confirm=true actually rescales the camera;
confirm=false just records candidates for review without adopting them.

groups.title: ② VLM Scale Cues — local vision model detects known-size objects (needs a running server)
③ Apply — confirm=true rescales; confirm=false only records candidates
